channel.title: Mysql on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions
channel.description: Recent content in Mysql on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions

item.title: AWS RDS Error: DB Instance Not Found / Cannot Connect
item.description:
The DBInstanceNotFound or connection failure errors occur when an RDS database instance is not in an available state, the endpoint is wrong, or network/security group rules block the connection.

Common Causes

  • The DB instance is in stopped state or was deleted
  • The security group does not allow inbound traffic on the database port
  • The instance is in a private subnet with no route to the client
  • The master password was rotated and the application has stale credentials
  • The DB instance is in a different VPC than the client

How to Fix

Check the DB instance status:

item.title: [Solution] PHP mysql_* Deprecated — Replace with mysqli_* Migration
item.description:
[Solution] PHP mysql_* Deprecated — Replace with mysqli_* Migration

The original mysql_* extension was deprecated in PHP 5.5 and removed in PHP 7.0. It was replaced by mysqli_* (MySQL Improved) and PDO (PHP Data Objects), both of which support prepared statements, which are critical for preventing SQL injection. Migrating is mandatory — your code will not run on PHP 7.0+ without updating.

What You’ll See

On PHP 7.0+:

Fatal error: Uncaught Error: Call to undefined function mysql_connect()

On PHP 5.5 through 5.6 (deprecated mode):
